items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: Supervised learning trains on fixed input–label pairs; RL learns from sequential interaction where rewards—not per-step labels—define success.

items.title: Machine learning umbrella
items.description: RL is one major learning paradigm alongside supervised, unsupervised, and self-supervised approaches within classical ML.

items.title: RLHF pipeline (SFT → RM → RL)
items.description: RLHF treats a language model as a policy and optimizes it with reward signals from human preferences—the same agent–reward–policy framing at LLM scale.

items.title: Agent fundamentals
items.description: Production AI agents combine policies, tools, and feedback loops; RL supplies the foundational vocabulary for learning from outcomes rather than static labels.

items.title: Unsupervised learning
items.description: Unsupervised methods discover structure without task rewards; RL is explicitly goal-directed through a reward function shaping behavior.

items.title: Self-supervised learning
items.description: Self-supervised learning creates training targets from data transformations; RL targets arise from environment interaction and delayed consequences.
